CANDIDATE 3
generation_source: llm_variant (label: llm_variant_2)
raw_model_response: (not captured for this source)
extracted_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT COUNT("products"."product_id") AS "product_count" FROM "products" INNER JOIN "inventory" ON "products"."product_id" = "inventory"."product_id" INNER JOIN "suppliers" ON "products"."supplier_id" = "suppliers"."supplier_id" WHERE "suppliers"."risk_level" = ? AND "inventory"."quantity_available" <= ?
normalized_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT COUNT("products"."product_id") AS "product_count" FROM "products" INNER JOIN "inventory" ON "products"."product_id" = "inventory"."product_id" INNER JOIN "suppliers" ON "products"."supplier_id" = "suppliers"."supplier_id" WHERE "suppliers"."risk_level" = ? AND "inventory"."quantity_available" <= ?
-- validation ------------------------------------------
{
  "parse_success": true,
  "parser_error": null,
  "referenced_tables": [
    "inventory",
    "products",
    "suppliers"
  ],
  "referenced_columns": [
    "inventory.product_id",
    "inventory.quantity_available",

    "products.supplier_id",
    "suppliers.risk_level",
    "suppliers.supplier_id"
  ],
  "detected_join_conditions": [
    "\"products\".\"product_id\" = \"inventory\".\"product_id\"",
    "\"products\".\"supplier_id\" = \"suppliers\".\"supplier_id\""
  ],

    "COUNT(\"products\".\"product_id\") AS \"product_count\""
  ],

LAYER 9 — FINAL DECISION
selected_candidate_number: 6
selected_candidate_label: llm_sql_direct_variant
selected_sql:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, p.sale_price
FROM products p
WHERE p.sale_price > (
    SELECT AVG(p2.sale_price)
    FROM products p2
    WHERE p2.category = p.category
)
exact_selection_reason: consensus_group
rejected_candidate_reasons:
{
  "llm_primary": [],
  "llm_variant_1": [
    "bare CTE 'category_avg_prices' never applies the comparison",
    "grain violation: the required aggregate comparison is never applied in WHERE or HAVING (contract requires comparing AVG(products.sale_price) per products.category, but no comparison predicate uses the measure products.sale_price)"
  ],
  "llm_variant_2": [],
  "llm_sql_direct": [],
  "llm_sql_direct_grain": [],
  "llm_sql_direct_variant": []
}

LAYERS 5-8 — CANDIDATES (generation, validation, execution, scoring)
candidate_count: 6

CANDIDATE 1
generation_source: llm_primary (label: llm_primary)
raw_model_response: (not captured for this source)
extracted_sql:
SELECT "employees"."department_id", "employees"."employee_id", "employees"."first_name", "employees"."last_name", "employees"."salary", AVG("employees"."salary") AS "avg_salary" FROM "employees" INNER JOIN "departments" ON "employees"."department_id" = "departments"."department_id" GROUP BY "employees"."department_id" HAVING "avg_salary" < "salary"
normalized_sql:
SELECT "employees"."department_id", "employees"."employee_id", "employees"."first_name", "employees"."last_name", "employees"."salary", AVG("employees"."salary") AS "avg_salary" FROM "employees" INNER JOIN "departments" ON "employees"."department_id" = "departments"."department_id" GROUP BY "employees"."department_id" HAVING "avg_salary" < "salary"

FROM shipments s
WHERE s.shipping_cost > (
    SELECT AVG(s2.shipping_cost)

    WHERE s2.carrier = s.carrier
)

raw_model_response: SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, w.warehouse_id, w.warehouse_name
FROM products p
JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id
JOIN warehouses w ON soi.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE s.rating = 1
  AND so.fulfillment_status = 'delivered'
  AND strftime('%Y', so.order_date) = '2025'
GROUP BY p.product_id, w.warehouse_id
HAVING SUM(soi.line_total) > 0;

JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id
JOIN warehouses w ON soi.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE s.rating = 1
  AND strftime('%Y', so.order_date) = '2025'
  AND so.fulfillment_status = 'delivered'
GROUP BY p.product_id, w.warehouse_id
HAVING SUM(soi.line_total) > 0;

SELECT "sales_order_items"."warehouse_id", "products"."product_id", "products"."product_name", SUM("sales_order_items"."line_total") AS "revenue" FROM "products" INNER JOIN "sales_order_items" ON "products"."product_id" = "sales_order_items"."product_id" INNER JOIN "sales_orders" ON "sales_order_items"."order_id" = "sales_orders"."order_id" INNER JOIN "suppliers" ON "products"."supplier_id" = "suppliers"."supplier_id" WHERE "suppliers"."rating" = ? AND "sales_orders"."order_status" = ? AND "sales_orders"."order_date" >= ? AND "sales_orders"."order_date" < ? GROUP BY "sales_order_items"."warehouse_id", "products"."product_id", "products"."product_name" ORDER BY "revenue" DESC

raw_model_response: SELECT a.city
FROM addresses a
JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id

HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY AVG(so.grand_total) DESC
LIMIT 5;

selected_sql:
SELECT a.city
FROM addresses a
JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
WHERE so.order_status = 'delivered'
GROUP BY a.city
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY AVG(so.grand_total) DESC
LIMIT 5
exact_selection_reason: consensus_group
rejected_candidate_reasons:
{
  "llm_primary": [],
  "llm_variant_1": [
    "fanout violation: AVG(\"sales_orders\".\"grand_total\") is aggregated after a one-to-many join multiplies its rows (many side: ['sales_order_items']); pre-aggregate per entity in its own CTE, use EXISTS for qualification, or COUNT(DISTINCT an entity key) [sales_orders 1->N sales_order_items (via sales_order_items.order_id)]"
  ],
  "llm_variant_2": [],
  "llm_sql_direct": [],
  "llm_sql_direct_grain": [],
  "llm_sql_direct_variant": []
}

CANDIDATE 3
generation_source: llm_sql_direct (label: llm_sql_direct)
raw_model_response: SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name, SUM(e.salary) * 1.0 / d.headcount_current AS salary_cost_per_headcount
FROM departments d
JOIN employees e ON d.department_id = e.department_id
GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name, d.headcount_current
HAVING d.headcount_current > 0;
extracted_sql:
SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name, SUM(e.salary) * 1.0 / d.headcount_current AS salary_cost_per_headcount
FROM departments d
JOIN employees e ON d.department_id = e.department_id
GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name, d.headcount_current
HAVING d.headcount_current > 0
normalized_sql:
SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name, SUM(e.salary) * 1.0 / d.headcount_current AS salary_cost_per_headcount
FROM departments d
JOIN employees e ON d.department_id = e.department_id
GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name, d.headcount_current
HAVING d.headcount_current > 0

selected_candidate_label: llm_sql_direct_variant
selected_sql:
SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name, AVG(e.performance_rating) / (AVG(e.salary) / 10000.0) AS performance_per_10k_salary
FROM departments d
JOIN employees e ON d.department_id = e.department_id
GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name

CANDIDATE 7
generation_source: llm_sql_repair (label: llm_sql_repair)
raw_model_response: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.product_id)
FROM products p
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id
WHERE so.sales_channel IN ('online', 'store')
GROUP BY p.product_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT so.sales_channel) = 2

LAYER 9 — FINAL DECISION
selected_candidate_number: 5
selected_candidate_label: llm_sql_direct_grain
selected_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT product_id)
FROM sales_order_items
WHERE product_id IN (
    SELECT product_id
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id
    WHERE so.sales_channel = 'online'
)
AND product_id IN (
    SELECT product_id
    FROM sales_order_items soi
    JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id
    WHERE so.sales_channel = 'store'
)

raw_model_response: SELECT c.customer_segment
FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id

CANDIDATE 6
generation_source: llm_sql_repair (label: llm_sql_repair)
raw_model_response: WITH segment_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        c.customer_segment,
        SUM(p.refunded_amount) AS total_refunded,
        SUM(p.amount) AS total_amount
    FROM customers c
    JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
    JOIN payments p ON so.order_id = p.order_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_segment
)
SELECT customer_segment
FROM segment_stats
WHERE total_amount > 0
  AND (total_refunded * 1.0 / total_amount) > 0.05;
extracted_sql:
WITH segment_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        c.customer_segment,
        SUM(p.refunded_amount) AS total_refunded,
        SUM(p.amount) AS total_amount
    FROM customers c
    JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
    JOIN payments p ON so.order_id = p.order_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_segment
)
SELECT customer_segment
FROM segment_stats
WHERE total_amount > 0
  AND (total_refunded * 1.0 / total_amount) > 0.05
normalized_sql:
WITH segment_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        c.customer_segment,
        SUM(p.refunded_amount) AS total_refunded,
        SUM(p.amount) AS total_amount
    FROM customers c
    JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
    JOIN payments p ON so.order_id = p.order_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_segment
)
SELECT customer_segment
FROM segment_stats
WHERE total_amount > 0
  AND (total_refunded * 1.0 / total_amount) > 0.05

WITH segment_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        c.customer_segment,

        SUM(p.amount) AS total_amount

CANDIDATE 4

raw_model_response: SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM (
    SELECT s.supplier_id
    FROM suppliers s
    JOIN products p ON s.supplier_id = p.supplier_id
    JOIN inventory i ON p.product_id = i.product_id
    GROUP BY s.supplier_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT i.warehouse_id) >= 3
)

LAYER 9 — FINAL DECISION
selected_candidate_number: 3
selected_candidate_label: llm_sql_direct
selected_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
FROM customers c
JOIN addresses a ON c.customer_id = a.customer_id
JOIN warehouses w ON a.city = w.city
WHERE a.is_primary = '1'
  AND w.operating_status = 'active'

SELECT "products"."product_id", "products"."product_name", "suppliers"."state_code", "warehouses"."state_code" FROM "products" INNER JOIN "inventory" ON "products"."product_id" = "inventory"."product_id" INNER JOIN "suppliers" ON "products"."supplier_id" = "suppliers"."supplier_id" INNER JOIN "warehouses" ON "inventory"."warehouse_id" = "warehouses"."warehouse_id"

WHERE s.state_code = w.state_code;

FROM customers c
JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id
JOIN payments p ON so.order_id = p.order_id
WHERE c.loyalty_tier = 'gold'
  AND so.fulfillment_status = 'delivered'
normalized_sql:

  AND so.fulfillment_status = 'delivered'

raw_model_response: SELECT 
    so.order_number,
    p.product_name,
    pay.amount,
    s.carrier
FROM sales_orders so
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON so.order_id = soi.order_id
JOIN products p ON soi.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN payments pay ON so.order_id = pay.order_id
JOIN shipments s ON so.order_id = s.order_id
WHERE so.order_status = 'delivered'
  AND so.sales_channel = 'online'
  AND p.category = 'Electronics';

LAYER 9 — FINAL DECISION
selected_candidate_number: 6
selected_candidate_label: llm_sql_direct_variant
selected_sql:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name
FROM products p
JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id
JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id
JOIN customers c ON so.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE p.product_id IN (
    SELECT p2.product_id
    FROM products p2
    JOIN sales_order_items soi2 ON p2.product_id = soi2.product_id
    JOIN sales_orders so2 ON soi2.order_id = so2.order_id
    JOIN customers c2 ON so2.customer_id = c2.customer_id
    WHERE c2.loyalty_tier IN ('gold', 'platinum')
    GROUP BY p2.product_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c2.loyalty_tier = 'gold' THEN c2.customer_id END) > 0
       AND COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c2.loyalty_tier = 'platinum' THEN c2.customer_id END) > 0
)
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM sales_order_items soi3
    JOIN sales_orders so3 ON soi3.order_id = so3.order_id
    JOIN customers c3 ON so3.customer_id = c3.customer_id
    WHERE soi3.product_id = p.product_id
      AND c3.loyalty_tier = 'bronze'
)
